description: Near the early part of its growth, an organism or developing thing is 'limu'. A young tree and a young person are still becoming, and the stage says nothing by itself about worth or ability. 'seroli' (mature) marks full development, while 'serao' (old) concerns long duration. 'helia' (youth) is the period or a young person rather than the quality.
articulatory_notes: Touch the tongue tip to the alveolar ridge for voiced 'l' and let air pass along the sides into stressed high-front unrounded 'i'. Close the lips for voiced nasal 'm', keep the voice in the nose, then raise the back of the tongue and round the lips for high-back 'u'.
sound_symbolism: The 'li' opening places 'limu' beside Phi's audible life family. After the lateral flow, closed lips and rounded 'u' give the word a small, gathered finish.

semantic_domains.temporal: a position near the beginning of a life or developmental course
